questions.stem: In the cell cycle, what is prophase?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the part of interphase during which DNA is replicated

choices.label: B
choices.text: the phase when chromosomes align at the cell's equator

choices.label: C
choices.text: the period of growth and DNA replication between divisions

choices.label: D
choices.text: the phase when chromosomes condense and the spindle begins to form

questions.explanation: prophase is the phase when chromosomes condense and the spindle begins to form.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: A red blood cell is placed in pure (distilled) water. What is the most likely result?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The cell begins photosynthesis

choices.label: B
choices.text: No net water movement occurs

choices.label: C
choices.text: Water leaves the cell, which shrinks

choices.label: D
choices.text: Water enters the cell, which swells and may burst

questions.explanation: Distilled water is hypotonic to the cell, so water moves in by osmosis, causing the cell to swell and potentially lyse.
